data.title: Aztreonam for Pharyngeal Gonorrhea: A Demonstration Study
data.brief_summary: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention has identified antimicrobial-resistant (AMR) Neisseria gonorrhoeae (NG) as one of the nation's top three urgent AMR threats. Since the advent of antibiotics in the 1930s, NG has developed resistance to every first-line antibiotic. Parenteral third-generation cephalosporins are now the only class of drug with consistent efficacy against NG. New therapies are urgently needed. Although some novel antimicrobials are under development, reevaluating older
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Persons diagnosed with pharyngeal gonorrhea or gonococcal urethritis who are undergoing pharyngeal gonorrhea testing, who are not yet treated.

Exclusion Criteria:

* Age less than 16 years
* Receipt of antibiotics in ≤30 days

endpoints: Treatment Efficacy of Pharyngeal Gonorrhea as Defined as the Proportion of Study Participants With Positive Pharyngeal Gonorrhea Culture at of Enrollment Whose Test of Cure Pharyngeal Culture is Negative
